concepts.title: Author's Purpose
concepts.description: The author's purpose in 'The Tiger King' is to highlight the importance of conservation and the role of humanity in it. It also explores the theme of human-tiger conflict and the consequences of our actions on the environment.

concepts.title: Tiger's Habitat
concepts.description: Tigers are found in various habitats, including forests, grasslands, and mangrove swamps. They prefer habitats with dense vegetation, water sources, and prey availability.

concepts.title: Tiger's Diet
concepts.description: Tigers are carnivores and primarily feed on large ungulates, such as deer and wild boar. They also hunt smaller prey, like monkeys and birds.

concepts.title: Human-Tiger Conflict
concepts.description: The conflict between humans and tigers arises due to habitat encroachment, prey depletion, and human activities like poaching, logging, and agriculture. This leads to tiger attacks on humans and livestock, causing significant losses.

concepts.title: Tiger's Striped Pattern
concepts.description: The stripes on a tiger's fur act as camouflage, helping them blend into their surroundings. Each tiger's pattern is unique, like human fingerprints, and can be used to identify individual animals.

concepts.title: Tiger's Social Structure
concepts.description: Tigers are solitary animals and do not live in groups like lions or wolves. They have a large territory, which they mark with their scent to keep other tigers away.

concepts.title: Tiger's Reproduction
concepts.description: Tigers give birth to 2-4 cubs after a gestation period of around 100 days. The cubs are born blind and dependent on their mother for food and protection. They become independent at around 2 years old.

concepts.title: Tiger's Conservation Status
concepts.description: Tigers are listed as endangered on the IUCN Red List due to habitat loss, poaching, and human-tiger conflict. Conservation efforts are underway to protect their habitats and populations.
